question: What is the total appropriation requested in the FY 2026-27 mid-year budget adjustment, per Item 9?
context:
Item 9. FY 2026-27 Mid-Year Budget Adjustment.
Staff recommends Council appropriate $2,450,000 from the General Fund unassigned reserve to cover (a) emergency storm drain repairs ($1,800,000) and (b) Public Works overtime ($650,000).

question: How many letters of opposition were received on the short-term rental ordinance, per the public comment summary?
context:
Public Comment Summary — Short-Term Rental Ordinance (Item 12)
Written comments received as of agenda publication: 47 in support, 113 in opposition, 9 neutral. Speaker cards filed for in-person comment: 22.

question: Per General Plan Goal LU-3, what is the city's policy on mixed-use development along El Camino Real?
context:
GENERAL PLAN — LAND USE ELEMENT
Goal LU-3: Encourage mixed-use development along El Camino Real to revitalize the corridor.
Policy LU-3.1: Allow ground-floor commercial with residential above, up to 3 stories.
Policy LU-3.2: Require pedestrian-oriented design and street-facing entries.
gold_answer: Encourage mixed-use development along El Camino Real to revitalize the corridor

question: What was the vote tally on the motion to approve the consent calendar at the February 18 meeting, per the minutes?
context:
MINUTES — Regular Meeting, February 18, 2026
Item 4. Consent Calendar.
Motion: Councilmember Ramirez moved to approve. Seconded by Mayor Pro Tem Chen.
Vote: 5-0 (Ayes: Ramirez, Chen, Patel, Goldstein, Mayor Whitfield).

question: What CEQA finding does the staff report recommend for the General Plan amendment GPA 25-002?
context:
STAFF REPORT — GPA 25-002
Environmental Review: Staff has determined the project is exempt from CEQA under State CEQA Guidelines § 15061(b)(3) (common sense exemption), as it can be seen with certainty that there is no possibility the activity may have a significant effect on the environment.
gold_answer: Exempt from CEQA under § 15061(b)(3), the common sense exemption

question: What is the deadline for filing a written appeal of the Planning Commission's decision, per Item 7?
context:
Item 7. Notice of Decision — Design Review DR 25-118.
The Planning Commission's decision is final unless appealed in writing to the City Clerk within 10 calendar days of the date of decision (March 5, 2026). Appeal fee: $385.
gold_answer: Within 10 calendar days of March 5, 2026 — i.e. by March 15, 2026

question: What is the contract amount and term recommended in the staff report for the street sweeping services agreement?
context:
STAFF REPORT — Street Sweeping Services Agreement
Recommendation: Award a 3-year agreement to ClearStreet Services, Inc. for an amount not to exceed $1,275,000 over the term, with two optional one-year renewals at the City Manager's discretion.
gold_answer: $1,275,000 over a 3-year term, with two optional one-year renewals

question: What is the maximum building height permitted in the SF-3 zoning district per the Austin Land Development Code excerpt?
context:
AUSTIN LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE — § 25-2-492
SF-3 Family Residence District.
(A) Site Development Regulations:
    (1) Minimum lot size: 5,750 sq ft.
    (2) Maximum building height: 35 feet.
    (3) Maximum floor-to-area ratio (FAR): 0.40.
    (4) Maximum impervious cover: 45%.

question: What does Resolution R-2026-058 from the Cuyahoga County, Ohio agenda authorize?
context:
CUYAHOGA COUNTY COUNCIL — Resolution R-2026-058
A RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE COUNTY EXECUTIVE TO ENTER INTO A CONTRACT WITH NORTHCOAST ENVIRONMENTAL LLC IN AN AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED $4,200,000 FOR REMEDIATION SERVICES AT THE FORMER METALS PROCESSING SITE LOCATED AT 1820 INDUSTRIAL PKWY.
gold_answer: Authorizes a contract with Northcoast Environmental LLC up to $4,200,000 for remediation at 1820 Industrial Pkwy

question: What was the vote tally on the New York City Council Intro 0412-2026 according to the minutes?
context:
NEW YORK CITY COUNCIL — Stated Meeting Minutes, March 12, 2026
Intro 0412-2026 (in relation to small-business permit waivers in flood-recovery zones).
Vote: 41 in favor, 7 opposed, 3 absent. Approved.
Members in opposition: CMs Donnelly, Ramirez-Hayes, Park, Liu, Patel, Whitaker, Carmichael.

question: What is the term length and renewal structure for the Austin city manager contract per the agenda item?
context:
ITEM 4 — Employment Agreement, City Manager
The attached agreement provides for an initial 4-year term, commencing May 1, 2026, with two optional 2-year renewals at the discretion of the Council. Annual base salary: $375,000.
gold_answer: 4 years initial, two optional 2-year renewals at Council discretion

question: What environmental review process applies to the Brooklyn rezoning per the staff report?
context:
NYC DEPARTMENT OF CITY PLANNING — Brooklyn Bridge South Rezoning Staff Report
CEQR No.: 26DCP034K
Environmental Review: A Final Environmental Impact Statement (FEIS) was issued under the City Environmental Quality Review (CEQR) procedure on February 12, 2026. SEQRA findings adopted concurrently.
gold_answer: FEIS under CEQR (CEQR No. 26DCP034K), issued February 12, 2026, with concurrent SEQRA findings

question: Per the Phoenix general plan amendment, what is the new maximum residential density?
context:
PHOENIX GENERAL PLAN AMENDMENT — GPA 26-014
Location: 1.8-acre parcel at 7th Ave & Indian School Rd.
Existing designation: Medium Density Residential (10 du/ac).
Proposed designation: High Density Residential (25 du/ac).
Finding: Consistent with the Reinvent Phoenix transit-oriented corridor objectives.
gold_answer: 25 dwelling units per acre (High Density Residential)

question: What action does Seattle Council Bill 121-2026 take on minimum-wage enforcement?
context:
SEATTLE COUNCIL BILL 121-2026
AN ORDINANCE relating to enforcement of the minimum wage and minimum compensation provisions of Title 14 SMC; expanding the Office of Labor Standards' authority to issue civil penalties up to $20,000 per violation; and adding new investigation timelines.
gold_answer: Expands Office of Labor Standards authority to issue civil penalties up to $20,000 per violation

question: What is the assessed valuation cap proposed in the Travis County school-district item?
context:
AUSTIN ISD BOARD AGENDA — Item 7B
Proposed Resolution: Adopt a homestead exemption increase of 20% (from $40,000 to $48,000) for the 2026-2027 tax year, subject to TEC § 11.13 limits and TCAD certification.
gold_answer: Increase from $40,000 to $48,000 for the 2026-2027 tax year

question: What deadline does the Atlanta zoning notice give for written objections?

gold_answer: 5:00 PM on April 7, 2026 (48 hours before the April 9 hearing)
